╭──(assassin@archhp)[~/vardio][2026/03/18][16:54:13]
╰─$

Why MED is Used

Spell checking
Autocorrect systems
Finding similarity between words
Search engines
Error correction in NLP

PDF Example: User types "recieve" → system finds "receive" using MED

The Formulas (from PDF)
Column Initialization (when target string is empty):

dist(i, 0) = dist(i−1, 0) + ins_cost(target(i))

Row Initialization (when source string is empty):

dist(0, j) = dist(0, j−1) + del_cost(source(j))

Main Formula:

dist(i,j) = min of:

dist(i−1, j) + ins_cost
dist(i−1, j−1) + sub_cost
dist(i, j−1) + del_cost



Algorithm (from PDF)
MIN-EDIT-DISTANCE(target, source):
  n ← LENGTH(target)
  m ← LENGTH(source)
  Create distance matrix distance[n+1, m+1]
  
  distance[0,0] ← 0
  
  for i from 1 to n:
    distance[i,0] ← distance[i−1,0] + ins_cost(target[i])
  
  for j from 1 to m:
    distance[0,j] ← distance[0,j−1] + del_cost(source[j])
  
  for i from 1 to n:
    for j from 1 to m:
      distance[i,j] ← MIN(
        distance[i−1,j] + ins_cost(target[i-1]),
        distance[i−1,j−1] + sub_cost(source[j-1], target[i-1]),
        distance[i,j−1] + del_cost(source[j-1])
      )
  
  return distance[n,m]

Handwritten Table from PDF (EXECUTION → EXECUTION)
The notebook page shows a full MED matrix being computed between two words. The handwritten calculations at the bottom show:
d(1,1):

= min(dist(0,1)+ins, dist(0,0)+sub, dist(1,0)+del)
= min(1+1, 0+1, 1+1)
= min(2,1,2) = 1

d(2,1):

= min(dis(1,1)+1, dis(1,0)+1, dis(2,0)+1)
= min(1+1, 1+1, 2+1)
= min(2,2,3) = 2

d(3,1):

= min(dis(2,1)+1, dis(2,0)+1, dis(3,0)+1)
= min(2+1, 2+1, 3+1)
= min(3,3,4) = 3

d(4,1):

= min(dis(3,1)+1, dis(3,0)+0, dis(4,0)+1)
= min(3+1, 3+0, 4+1)
= min(4,3,5) = 3

Final answer shown in the matrix = 5 (bottom-right cell)

1757. Recyclable and Low Fat Products
Easy
Topics
premium lock iconCompanies
SQL Schema
Pandas Schema

Table: Products

+-------------+---------+
| Column Name | Type    |
+-------------+---------+
| product_id  | int     |
| low_fats    | enum    |
| recyclable  | enum    |
+-------------+---------+
product_id is the primary key (column with unique values) for this table.
low_fats is an ENUM (category) of type ('Y', 'N') where 'Y' means this product is low fat and 'N' means it is not.
recyclable is an ENUM (category) of types ('Y', 'N') where 'Y' means this product is recyclable and 'N' means it is not.

 

Write a solution to find the ids of products that are both low fat and recyclable.

Return the result table in any order.

The result format is in the following example.

 

Example 1:

Input: 
Products table:
+-------------+----------+------------+
| product_id  | low_fats | recyclable |
+-------------+----------+------------+
| 0           | Y        | N          |
| 1           | Y        | Y          |
| 2           | N        | Y          |
| 3           | Y        | Y          |
| 4           | N        | N          |
+-------------+----------+------------+
Output: 
+-------------+
| product_id  |
+-------------+
| 1           |
| 3           |
+-------------+
Explanation: Only products 1 and 3 are both low fat and recyclable.

rm ~/.config/pipewire/pipewire.conf.d/firefox-audio.conf
systemctl --user restart pipewire pipewire-pulse
sleep 3

cat > ~/.config/pipewire/pipewire.conf.d/firefox-audio.conf << 'EOF'
context.objects = [
  {
    factory = spa-node-factory
    args = {
      factory.name     = alsa
      node.name        = "Firefox Audio"
      node.description = "Firefox Audio Output"
      media.class      = "Audio/Sink"
      audio.format     = "S32LE"
      audio.rate       = 48000
      audio.channels   = 2
      alsa.device      = "front:1"
    }
  }
]
EOF
